name: Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate - Part Three: The Emerald Village

text.content:
Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate

Part Three: The Emerald Village

The Thrilling Conclusion of the Three-Part Campaign

Location: Black Forest, Center Germany - The Village of Smaragd

Duration: 4-5 Sessions

What Happened Before

The Hunters have pursued OZena and Dr. Henkle through two villages:

  • Part One: Gelb, where Margareta the Good Witch protected outcasts
  • Part Two: Rot, where Helga the Red Witch ruled through blood magic

Now they reach Smaragd, the Emerald Village at the heart of the Black Forest, where Dr. Norman Henkle has established himself as a powerful wizard. Here, the truth about the Compass Rose, the manipulation of OZena and her companions, and Henkle's ultimate plan will be revealed.

The Situation

Dr. Henkle has fortified Smaragd using a magical wishing well that grants twisted versions of people's desires. The villagers have been transformed into monsters by their own wishes. Henkle is constructing a hot-air balloon to escape with the Compass Rose pieces, and his three guardians—Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose—have been transformed by their own character development.

Depending on how Parts One and Two concluded:

  • Dorothea (OZena) may be an ally or an enemy
  • Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose may be hostile or willing to negotiate
  • The Hunters may have 0-3 pieces of the Compass Rose

The Story of Smaragd

When Dr. Norman Henkle arrived in 1780 with his patients, he found himself in Smaragd—a village suffering from a terrible epidemic. Using his knowledge of future medicine, particularly penicillin (which wouldn't be discovered for another 150 years), Henkle cured the epidemic and was hailed as a miracle worker.

The grateful villagers made him their leader. Henkle discovered an ancient wishing well in the forest nearby—a magical artifact left by a forgotten wizard. The well could grant wishes, but in twisted, corrupted ways.

Henkle experimented with the well, learning its rules:

  • It grants the literal interpretation of wishes, often with horrifying results
  • Each wish granted strengthens the well's power
  • The well is somehow connected to the Compass Rose

Henkle used the well to transform Smaragd into his fortress. He manipulated villagers into making wishes that would serve his purposes, creating an army of monsters. He also:

  • Drove out or killed Griselda, the fourth witch who held the central piece
  • Began constructing a hot-air balloon for escape
  • Manipulated Dorothea into murdering the other witches
  • Kept Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose loyal through promises and lies

Henkle's Plan

Henkle intends to activate the incomplete Compass Rose (he has only 2 of 4 pieces, possibly 3 if he captured Dorothea's) and use it to:

  • Return to his own time as a powerful wizard
  • Or conquer this time period with future knowledge
  • Or both, moving between eras at will

He knows the incomplete Compass Rose is unstable and dangerous, but he doesn't care about the consequences. The activation will tear holes in reality and throw people randomly through time and space—but if Henkle is in his hot-air balloon when it happens, he believes he can control where he goes.

What Henkle Doesn't Know

The entire situation—including his discovery of the Compass Rose, the time displacement, and even the wishing well's location—was orchestrated by the One Who Plots in the Shadows. Henkle is just another pawn in a larger game.

FIRST SCENE - Approaching Smaragd

The approach to Smaragd has been transformed into a death trap. A massive Pit surrounds the village—a moat-like excavation 30 feet deep with smooth walls that are nearly impossible to climb.

The Pit

The Pit is a Threat with OL Medium 5 to navigate. Hunters must make a Check to:

  • Climb down safely (failure means falling and taking 1 Wound)
  • Find a safe crossing point
  • Scale the opposite wall

The Pit was created by villagers who wished to "protect our home from outsiders." Their wish was granted by making them dig obsessively until they created this barrier, then they transformed into the creatures that now guard it.

The Ambush

As the Hunters navigate the Pit, they're ambushed by Henkle's forces. Depending on the story so far:

If Dorothea is an enemy: She appears with Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose, all three transformed by their character arcs. They attack to prevent the Hunters from reaching Henkle.

If Dorothea is an ally: She warns the Hunters about the ambush and helps them prepare. Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose appear but may be willing to negotiate if Dorothea vouches for the Hunters.

If Dorothea is dead or captured: Only Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose appear, filled with rage and grief. They blame the Hunters and attack without mercy.

The Transformed Companions

Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose have undergone dramatic transformations based on their character development:

  • Lowe the Coward has become Lowe the Brave Fighter
  • Hirnloser the Scarecrow has become Hirnloser the Genius
  • Dose the Tin Man has become Dose the Giant in Love

Each has gained new powers and confidence, making them far more dangerous than before.

The Transformed Companions

Lowe the Brave Fighter

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I was called a coward, but I have found my courage. I am a lion-faced warrior who stands firm when others flee. I will protect those I care about, no matter the cost.

Gift - Hero

Lowe has overcome his cowardice and embraced heroism:

  • Fearless: Lowe is immune to fear effects and mind control
  • Inspiring Presence: Allied NPCs within 30 feet gain +1 to Opposition Level
  • Last Stand: When reduced to 0 Wounds, Lowe can take one final action before falling

Hirnloser the Genius

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: They said I had no brain, but I have discovered that my mind is my greatest weapon. I see patterns others miss, solutions others cannot imagine. I am no scarecrow—I am a genius.

Gift - Genius

Hirnloser has realized his intellect is extraordinary:

  • Tactical Insight: Once per Scene, Hirnloser can describe a brilliant tactic that automatically succeeds
  • Countermeasure: Three times per Scene, Hirnloser can prevent a Hunter from activating a Gift by explaining how to counter it
  • Prepared: Hirnloser always has the right tool or item for the situation

Dose the Giant in Love

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I was hollow metal, feeling nothing. But I have fallen in love with a villager of Smaragd, and my heart has returned. Where love touches, flesh replaces steel. I am becoming human again.

Gift - Of Steel and Love

Dose's armor is slowly being replaced by living flesh wherever his beloved touches him:

  • Partial Immunity: Dose still has metal armor covering most of his body, providing immunity to physical attacks except where flesh is exposed
  • Vulnerable Heart: The area around his heart is now flesh, protected only by the small door. Attacking this spot requires 1 Increment but bypasses his armor
  • Love's Strength: When fighting to protect his beloved, Dose can act twice in one Exchange

Roleplaying the Companions

Lowe: No longer adapts to the winning side. He's chosen his loyalty (to Henkle or to the Hunters depending on Dorothea's fate) and stands firm. Speaks with confidence and meets eyes directly.

Hirnloser: No longer speaks in riddles. His speech is clear, precise, and brilliant. He rapidly analyzes situations and proposes solutions. Shows pride in his intellect.

Dose: Speaks about his beloved constantly. Shows patches of exposed flesh on his arms and chest. Moves more fluidly than before. Protective and emotional rather than mechanical and hollow.

SECOND SCENE - The Emerald Village

Smaragd itself has been transformed into a nightmarish labyrinth. The village streets constantly shift and change, creating an Illusory Labyrinth that traps intruders.

The Illusory Labyrinth

The Labyrinth is a Threat with OL Hard 7. The streets of Smaragd appear to change between night and day randomly:

  • During "Day": The streets look normal but lead in impossible directions
  • During "Night": The streets become even more twisted, with buildings appearing and disappearing

To navigate through requires a Check against OL Hard 7. Failure means the Hunters become lost and must try again, wasting time. Each failed attempt triggers another ambush.

The Villagers of Emerald

As the Hunters navigate the Labyrinth, they encounter the Villagers of Emerald—ordinary people transformed by the wishing well into monsters.

Each villager made a wish, and each wish was granted in the most literal, horrifying way possible. Roll 1d6+1 to determine what each villager wished for:

  1. Wealth: Spits gold coins and bile, hits 3 targets within 15 feet
  2. Beauty: Body warps and shifts constantly, viewers suffer 1 Penalty
  3. Health: Regenerates 1 Wound immediately, cannot die permanently
  4. Skill: Opposition Level increases to Medium 5
  5. To Never Sleep: Cannot sleep, chatters nonsense constantly, exhausting to deal with
  6. To Grow Up: Child with giant body, +2 Wounds total, inflicts +1 Wound damage
  7. Love: Merged with their beloved into one body, can act twice per Exchange

The Ambushes

Henkle has positioned his forces throughout the Labyrinth. The Hunters face multiple encounters:

  • Canine Tigers: Creatures that appear as dogs but fight like tigers when wounded
  • Wish-Warped Villagers: Groups of 3-5 villagers with different wishes
  • Possibly Lowe, Hirnloser, or Dose: If they weren't defeated in Scene One

Each combat encounter places 1 Time Token on the Hot-Air Balloon Gift (see Henkle's profile). When 3 Tokens accumulate, Henkle's balloon is ready to launch.

Villagers of Emerald

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPCs (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I was an ordinary villager who made a wish at the well. My wish was granted, but not in the way I hoped. Now I am a monster, and I serve the wizard who promised to fix everything.

Gift - Careful What You Wish For

Each Villager of Emerald has been transformed by their wish. Roll 1d6+1 to determine the effect:

  1. I Wished for Wealth: The villager's body produces gold coins internally. They can spit 1d6 gold coins at enemies, hitting up to 3 targets within 15 feet. Each hit inflicts 1 Wound. They also vomit bile constantly.
  2. I Wished for Beauty: The villager's body constantly warps and shifts, trying to achieve impossible beauty standards. Anyone looking at them suffers 1 Penalty on all Checks due to the disturbing sight.
  3. I Wished for Health: The villager cannot die or stay injured. They regenerate 1 Wound immediately at the start of each Exchange. They can only be permanently defeated by destroying the wishing well or using the Compass Rose to reverse the wish.
  4. I Wished for Skill: The villager becomes supernaturally skilled at their profession. Their Opposition Level increases to Medium 5. They perform their craft obsessively, even in combat.
  5. I Wished to Never Sleep: The villager can never sleep again. They're exhausted and manic, chattering nonsense constantly. Their speech is so annoying that anyone nearby suffers 1 Penalty on Checks requiring concentration.
  6. I Wished to Grow Up: A child whose body grew to giant size but whose mind remained childish. They have +2 Wounds total (3 instead of 1) and inflict +1 Wound damage. They cry and tantrum during combat.
  7. I Wished to Fall in Love: The villager and their beloved merged into one body. They can perform 2 actions per Exchange, but they argue constantly about what to do. If one "half" is convinced to stop fighting, the entire body stops.

Canine Tigers

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPCs (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: We appear as large dogs, but we were created by the wishing well to be guardians. We are docile until wounded—then we become fierce as tigers.

Gift - Fierce as Tiger

Canine Tigers appear docile and even friendly. But when a Canine Tiger suffers its first Wound:

  • Its Opposition Level immediately increases to Hard 7
  • It enters a rage and attacks the nearest living thing (ally or enemy)
  • It cannot be reasoned with or calmed
  • It fights until destroyed or until all nearby creatures flee

Storytellers can use Canine Tigers as a complication: they may initially appear to be potential allies or harmless creatures, only to become deadly threats once wounded.

THIRD SCENE - The Magician's Fake Castle

At the center of Smaragd stands what appears to be a magnificent emerald castle with towers and battlements. But as the Hunters approach, they realize it's an illusion—the "castle" is actually an enormous oak tree that's been carved, painted, and decorated to look like a fortress.

The Oak Tree Workshop

Inside the hollow oak tree, Dr. Henkle has established his workshop:

  • Tables covered with stolen books and notes
  • Alchemical equipment
  • Maps and diagrams of the Compass Rose
  • A large platform where the hot-air balloon is tethered
  • The balloon itself, inflated and ready (if 3 Time Tokens accumulated)

The Hunters can see the hot-air balloon inflating above the tree, its colorful fabric visible for miles. Henkle is preparing to launch.

The Final Ambush

Before the Hunters can confront Henkle, they face the last of his guardians:

  • Any surviving companions (Lowe, Hirnloser, Dose)
  • Dorothea, if she's still hostile
  • Canine Tigers and Wish-Warped Villagers
  • Possibly Griselda, the fourth witch, if she survived (Storyteller's choice)

This battle should be desperate and dramatic. The sounds of combat alert Henkle that the Hunters have broken through his defenses.

Evidence of Henkle's Crimes

While fighting or after defeating the guardians, the Hunters can find evidence in the workshop:

  • Henkle's journal detailing his manipulation of Dorothea
  • Notes about deliberately withholding cures to maintain control
  • Plans to abandon his patients once he escapes
  • Research stolen from others, including the original Compass Rose creator
  • Letters to a mysterious benefactor (the One Who Plots in the Shadows)

This evidence can be used to convince any remaining hostile NPCs that Henkle has betrayed everyone.

pages.name: The End of OZena's Story - Scene Four

text.content:
FOURTH SCENE - The End of OZena's Sad Story

The final confrontation takes place on the platform inside the oak tree, where Dr. Norman Henkle prepares to board his hot-air balloon and activate the incomplete Compass Rose.

Dr. Norman Henkle

Henkle appears as a charming man in his 40s, dressed in anachronistic clothing (a mix of 1900s and 1780s fashion). He carries himself with absolute confidence and speaks with a silver tongue that has convinced countless people to follow him.

When the Hunters arrive, Henkle is not surprised:

"Ah, the Order's agents. I must say, you've been more persistent than I anticipated. But you're too late. The Compass Rose will activate in moments, and I'll be free of this primitive era. You're welcome to join me, if you can afford the price."

The Confrontation

Henkle doesn't want to fight—he wants to escape. He offers deals:

  • Join him: Serve as his agents in the future, earn wealth and power
  • Let him go: He'll leave the Hunters the wishing well and Smaragd
  • Trade: He'll cure one person (Dorothea?) in exchange for free passage

If the Hunters refuse all deals, Henkle fights—but his primary goal is buying time for the balloon to launch.

Dorothea's Choice

If Dorothea is present (as ally or enemy), this is her moment of truth:

  • If she's an ally: She confronts Henkle about his lies and manipulation
  • If she's an enemy: Henkle tries to convince her to kill the Hunters
  • If she's absent: Her companions (if any survived) act in her memory

Henkle's response to Dorothea reveals his true nature: he's completely amoral and sees her only as a tool. This can be the catalyst for her final redemption or her tragic end.

The Compass Rose Activation

During the confrontation, Henkle activates the incomplete Compass Rose. The result is catastrophic:

  • Reality begins to tear and warp
  • Portals open randomly, showing different times and places
  • People near the Compass Rose are pulled toward the portals
  • The hot-air balloon begins to lift off

The Hunters must choose:

  • Stop Henkle before he escapes
  • Deactivate or destroy the Compass Rose
  • Save people being pulled into portals
  • All of the above (if they're clever)

Dr. Norman Henkle, The Powerful Wizard

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a brilliant researcher from the early 1900s, transported to 1780 where my future knowledge makes me a wizard. I will do whatever it takes to escape this primitive era and return to civilization—or perhaps conquer both times and rule as a god.

Agenda: Activate the Compass Rose and escape / Protect my research and stolen knowledge / Eliminate anyone who threatens my plans / Maintain my image as a benevolent wizard.

Gift - Hot-Air Balloon

Henkle has constructed a hot-air balloon using knowledge from his own time. The balloon represents his escape plan:

Time Tokens: During the Scenario, place 1 Time Token on this Gift each time:

  • A combat encounter occurs in Smaragd (max 1 per Scene)
  • The Hunters waste time being lost in the Labyrinth
  • Henkle successfully delays or distracts the Hunters

Ready to Launch: When 3 Time Tokens are on this Gift, the hot-air balloon is fully inflated and ready. Henkle can board it and launch as a single action.

Escape: Once airborne, Henkle is extremely difficult to stop. Hunters must use creative methods (shooting the balloon, magical effects, climbing the tether, etc.) to prevent his escape. If he escapes, he activates the Compass Rose while airborne, believing this will let him control his destination.

Special Ability - Silver Tongue

Henkle is a master manipulator who has convinced entire villages to follow him. When Henkle attempts to deceive, persuade, or manipulate, he has +2 to his Opposition Level (making it Hard 7).

NPCs are particularly vulnerable to his charisma. Any NPC who isn't a Hunter must make a Check against OL Hard 7 to resist believing his lies.

Weakness - Plagiarist

Henkle's genius is mostly stolen. He has surface knowledge of many subjects but deep understanding of none. If confronted with someone who truly understands a topic he's claiming expertise in, his façade crumbles and his Opposition Level drops to Easy 3 for that exchange.

Roleplaying Henkle

  • Voice: Smooth and charming with an American accent
  • Mannerisms: Constantly smiling, makes grand gestures, touches people to establish connection
  • Motivation: Pure self-interest disguised as benevolence
  • Tells: His smile doesn't reach his eyes; he checks escape routes constantly
  • When cornered: Becomes desperate and vicious, all charm vanishing

Epilogue: The Aftermath

Possible Endings

Henkle Escapes:

  • He activates the incomplete Compass Rose while in his balloon
  • The activation goes wrong—the balloon is thrown randomly through time
  • Henkle may end up anywhere from prehistoric times to the distant future
  • He becomes a recurring villain, appearing at different points in history
  • The Compass Rose pieces scatter across time and space

Henkle is Stopped:

  • He's captured and can be turned over to authorities or the Order
  • His research is secured, preventing others from repeating his mistakes
  • The Compass Rose pieces can be reunited or destroyed
  • Smaragd begins to return to normal as the wishing well's power fades

Henkle is Killed:

  • His death triggers the Compass Rose, creating a massive portal storm
  • Everyone in Smaragd risks being thrown through time
  • The Hunters must act quickly to stabilize or destroy the Compass Rose
  • Some villagers may be permanently trapped in their wish-transformed states

Dorothea's Fate

If She Survives and Redeems Herself:

  • The Hunters can take her back to their own time using a controlled Compass Rose
  • Or she chooses to stay in 1780, using her knowledge to help others
  • She may join the Order as a Hunter herself
  • She finds purpose in making amends for the witches she killed

If She Remains Hostile:

  • She attempts to activate the Compass Rose herself
  • She may sacrifice herself to stop Henkle
  • She's captured and must face justice for her murders
  • She escapes, becoming a recurring tragic antagonist

If She Didn't Survive:

  • Her companions (Lowe, Hirnloser, Dose) mourn her loss
  • They may choose to stay in 1780 or risk the Compass Rose to go home
  • Her story becomes a cautionary tale within the Order

The Companions' Resolution

Lowe the Brave: Having found his courage, Lowe may become a Hunter himself or return to his own time to live boldly. He thanks the Hunters for showing him that courage isn't the absence of fear—it's acting despite it.

Hirnloser the Genius: Having proven his intellect, Hirnloser may stay in 1780 to become a true inventor and scholar. Or he returns home to revolutionize early 20th century psychiatry with his insights about the human mind.

Dose the Beloved: Having found love, Dose chooses to stay in 1780 with his beloved. The Hunters witness the last of his metal armor falling away, leaving him fully human again. Love literally saved his humanity.

Concluding the Ozena Campaign

The Compass Rose's Final Fate

The Hunters must decide what to do with the Compass Rose pieces:

Reunite and Destroy: Bringing all four pieces together allows them to be destroyed permanently. This prevents anyone from ever misusing the artifact again, but also eliminates a potentially useful tool.

Separate and Hide: Following the four witches' example, scatter the pieces to different locations and guardians. This prevents misuse but risks future problems.

Give to the Order: Turn the pieces over to the Order of the Hidden Rose for safekeeping. The Order can study it and potentially use it for good—but also risks internal corruption.

Keep One Piece: A Hunter might keep a piece as a memento or ace-in-the-hole. This allows emergency time travel but risks temptation and theft.

The Wishing Well

The magical wishing well remains in the forest near Smaragd. The Hunters can:

  • Destroy it (preventing future harm, but also eliminating its potential benefits)
  • Seal it (make it inaccessible but preserve it)
  • Study it (risking its corruption spreading)
  • Make their own wishes (extremely dangerous)

Smaragd's Future

With Henkle gone, Smaragd faces an uncertain future:

  • The Wish-Warped Villagers may gradually return to normal
  • Or they remain permanently transformed, requiring care and acceptance
  • The village could become a new haven for outcasts (like Gelb was)
  • Or it could be abandoned as cursed and dangerous

Connection to the Greater Campaign

If the Hunters are playing through a larger Broken Tales campaign, Ozena connects to the overarching mystery of the One Who Plots in the Shadows:

  • Henkle's mysterious benefactor who provided the incomplete Compass Rose
  • The orchestration of the entire time-displacement event
  • The connection between the wishing well and fairy tale magic
  • Evidence that someone is manipulating events across multiple Scenarios

The Hunters may discover clues pointing to Borgo Maleanime, the hidden village in Northern Italy where the One Who Plots in the Shadows resides.

Scenario Events

  • If Henkle realizes he's missing Compass Rose pieces: He becomes desperate and offers increasingly unreasonable deals. He may threaten to activate the incomplete Rose anyway, consequences be damned.
  • If Dorothea confronts Henkle and he admits he manipulated her: She may attempt to kill him regardless of the Hunters' plans. The Hunters must decide whether to stop her or let justice be done.
  • If Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose are shown evidence of Henkle's betrayal: They turn against him immediately. Lowe is heartbroken but resolute, Hirnloser is furious at being fooled, Dose weeps for Dorothea.
  • If a Hunter makes a wish at the wishing well: The Storyteller should grant it in the most literal, twisted way possible. A wish to "save everyone" might freeze everyone in time forever.
  • If the Hunters destroy the Compass Rose: It explodes in a burst of temporal energy. Everyone present must make a Check against OL Hard 7 or be thrown to a random point in time (1d6: 1-2 = past, 3-4 = stay, 5-6 = future).
  • If the Hunters try to take Henkle alive: He attempts suicide rather than face capture, trying to take the Compass Rose with him.
  • If a Canine Tiger wounds a wish-warped villager: The Tiger's rage may break the wish's hold, reverting the villager to normal temporarily.
  • If the hot-air balloon is destroyed before Henkle can escape: He has a backup plan—a hidden tunnel leading to the wishing well. He'll try to wish himself to safety.
  • If all three companions survive and are redeemed: They offer to help the Hunters in future Scenarios. The Order gains three unusual but loyal allies from the past.
  • If the Hunters reunite all four Compass Rose pieces: They briefly see visions of the artifact's creator—a sad, ancient wizard warning them about the One Who Plots in the Shadows.

Rewards and Consequences

Experience and Growth

Completing the three-part Ozena campaign should provide significant character development:

  • Hunters gain 3-5 XP per Part (9-15 XP total)
  • Each Hunter should have opportunities to gain or modify Descriptors based on their experiences
  • Hunters may develop new relationships, allies, or enemies from the campaign
  • The moral complexity should challenge Hunters' beliefs and motivations

Physical Rewards

Depending on how the campaign concluded, Hunters might obtain:

  • Compass Rose Pieces: Powerful artifacts that can be used (carefully) for emergency time travel
  • Henkle's Research: Notes on time travel, alchemy, and manipulation that the Order values highly
  • The Silver Slippers: If Dorothea is defeated, her enchanted shoes can be recovered
  • Infusion of Purity Recipe: The formula for breaking blood magic curses
  • Future Knowledge: Information from 1900s that's valuable in 1780

Allies Gained

  • Dorothea Gray: A tragic figure seeking redemption
  • Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose: Three unusual but loyal companions
  • Survivors of Gelb and Rot: Outcasts who remember the Hunters' kindness
  • The Order: Increased reputation and trust within the organization

Enemies Made

  • Dr. Norman Henkle: If he escaped, he's a recurring villain across time
  • Helga's Cult: If she survived, her blood magic cult may seek revenge
  • Wish-Warped Villagers: Some may blame the Hunters for destroying their "gifts"
  • The One Who Plots: The true mastermind becomes aware of the Hunters

Setting Changes

The Ozena campaign can have lasting effects on the world:

  • Gelb: Becomes a permanent haven for outcasts, or falls into despair if Margareta died
  • Rot: Helga's death or redemption dramatically changes the village's nature
  • Smaragd: Returns to normalcy, becomes a cursed ruin, or transforms into something new
  • Time Anomalies: The Compass Rose's activation may create lingering temporal distortions

Long-Term Story Hooks

  • Henkle appearing in different time periods as a recurring villain
  • Other pieces of the Compass Rose scattered through time
  • Investigations into the One Who Plots in the Shadows
  • Dorothea's quest for redemption and finding a cure for ozena
  • The companions' integration into 1780 society or attempts to return home
  • Consequences of wish-magic spreading beyond Smaragd
  • The Order studying time travel and its implications

flags.setting: Black Forest, Germany - 1780 - The Village of Smaragd
